one_line_takeaway: LLM-generated persuasive rewordings of claims (especially vague, concreteness-stripping 'Obfuscation') defeat FEVER/FEVEROUS fact-checking pipelines far more effectively than standard lexical adversarial attacks, simultaneously breaking BM25 evidence retrieval and RoBERTa-based veracity classification, with an oracle attacker collapsing accuracy to near zero even given gold evidence.
key_finding: Persuasion attacks substantially outperform standard lexical/paraphrastic baselines at degrading AFC performance. Baseline attacks (synonym substitution, character perturbation, word swap, paraphrasing) reduced accuracy by at most 6 points (FEVER) and under 3.2 points (FEVEROUS). By contrast, the Blind persuasion attack dropped claim-only accuracy by 13.2 points on FEVER and 6.6 on FEVEROUS, and gold-evidence accuracy by roughly 17 points on both. Under the Oracle (worst-case) attacker, claim-only accuracy collapsed to 0.043 (FEVER) and 0.010 (FEVEROUS); even with gold-evidence, accuracy fell to 0.164 (FEVER) and 0.250 (FEVEROUS). Error analysis showed asymmetric vulnerability: gold-evidence models strongly suppress Evasion ASR under Blind attack (7% FEVER, 4% FEVEROUS) but are actually more vulnerable than claim-only models to Sabotage (Blind Sabotage ASR rises from <22% claim-only to >44% with gold-evidence). Under Oracle attack, Sabotage ASR reaches near-100% in both settings, and Evasion ASR reaches 75.9% (FEVER) / 43.4% (FEVEROUS) even with gold-evidence -- over a 10x increase versus Blind. At the technique level, Obfuscation (Manipulative Wording category) was the single most damaging technique, achieving both an average Evasion ASR of 27% (with gold-evidence) and the largest retrieval degradation (|Delta Recall@5| = 0.54), because replacing concrete entities with vague phrasing (e.g., '50 days' -> 'approximately one and a half months') simultaneously denies the BM25 retriever exact-match keywords and introduces classifier ambiguity. Slogan and Obfuscation achieved the highest claim-only evasion rates (>65%). Techniques in Attack-on-Reputation, Distraction, and Simplification categories collapsed to near-zero evasion once gold-evidence was provided, while Manipulative Wording, Call, and Justification techniques remained effective even under evidence grounding. On retrieval, the strongest lexical baseline (Character Noise) dropped Recall@5 by only 0.105 (FEVER)/0.023 (FEVEROUS), whereas Blind persuasion attacks dropped Recall@5 by 0.197 (FEVER) and 0.215 (FEVEROUS), with the Oracle setting collapsing Recall@k to near-zero at all depths.
core_contribution: The paper introduces 'persuasion injection attacks', the first adversarial framework that uses a generative LLM (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) to rewrite fact-checking claims with persuasion/propaganda techniques (drawn from Piskorski et al. 2023's 23-technique, 6-category taxonomy) in order to make Automated Fact-Checking (AFC) systems misclassify claims. Unlike prior adversarial-attack work on AFC that relies on surface-level noise (typos, synonym substitution) or generic LLM paraphrasing/semantic edits, this is (per the authors, and confirmed as such against the Related Work section) the first work to weaponize persuasive rhetoric specifically as an adversarial vector against AFC pipelines. The paper also proposes a decoupled evaluation methodology that separately measures the attack's effect on (1) the veracity classifier (in claim-only and gold-evidence settings) and (2) BM25 evidence retrieval, to disentangle which pipeline stage is being broken.
threat_model: Attacker crafts or rewrites a false (or true) claim using an LLM instructed to apply a specific persuasion technique, with the goal of causing an AFC pipeline (retriever + RoBERTa-base veracity classifier) to misclassify it -- either evasion (False claim predicted True, i.e., disinformation slipping past the fact-checker) or sabotage (True claim predicted False). Two attacker capability levels are studied: a Blind Attacker who applies a persuasion technique at random (average-case, no query access), and an Oracle Attacker with query access to the classifier who selects, per claim, whichever of the generated persuasive variants maximizes misclassification probability (worst-case). The attack is applied only to claims (not evidence), acts upstream of both retrieval and classification, and is evaluated on the FEVER and FEVEROUS benchmarks using a RoBERTa-base classifier trained by the authors (not a production AFC system).
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) study restricted to English; findings may not transfer to other languages without adaptation for linguistic/cultural variation; (2) experiments rely on Wikipedia-based benchmarks (FEVER/FEVEROUS), which may differ from real-world disinformation found in social media posts or news articles, so persuasion attacks may behave differently in those genres; (3) adversarial variants were generated using modest-sized (~7B parameter) LLMs, and technique effectiveness likely depends on generator capability -- more capable models might strengthen techniques that appeared weak here. Additional methodological caveats evident from the text: the AFC classifier is a RoBERTa-base model trained by the authors rather than a deployed production system, chosen specifically to avoid confounders (hallucination, prompt sensitivity, data contamination) that generative-LLM-based verifiers would introduce, so results may not directly generalize to LLM-based fact-checkers; label-invariance was validated by a single trained annotator (not multiple annotators/inter-annotator agreement reported) on a 690-claim sample; and BM25/Pyserini retrieval is a sparse, page-level baseline rather than the dense/hybrid retrievers used in some modern AFC pipelines, so retrieval degradation results may not transfer to those systems.

quantitative_results: Original (no attack) Gold-Evidence accuracy: FEVER 0.943, FEVEROUS 0.894
Original (no attack) Claim-Only accuracy: FEVER 0.814, FEVEROUS 0.641
Persuasion (Blind) claim-only accuracy drop: FEVER -0.132, FEVEROUS -0.066
Persuasion (Blind) gold-evidence accuracy drop: FEVER -0.179, FEVEROUS -0.168
Best baseline attack (accuracy drop) FEVER: at most 6 points claim-only, 3-10 points gold-evidence; FEVEROUS: below 3.2 points both settings
Evasion ASR (Blind): Claim-Only 30.6% FEVER / 31.8% FEVEROUS; Gold-Evidence 7.0% FEVER / 3.9% FEVEROUS
Evasion ASR (Oracle) with gold-evidence: 75.9% FEVER, 43.4% FEVEROUS
Sabotage ASR (Oracle): ~100% in both Claim-Only and Gold-Evidence settings

Obfuscation technique: Evasion ASR up to 73% (claim-only, FEVER) dropping to 38% (gold-evidence, FEVER) and 15% (gold-evidence, FEVEROUS); average Evasion ASR 27% with gold-evidence, |Delta Recall@5| 0.54

Recall@5 degradation: strongest lexical baseline (Character Noise) -0.105 (FEVER) / -0.023 (FEVEROUS); Persuasion Blind -0.197 (FEVER) / -0.215 (FEVEROUS); Persuasion Oracle collapses Recall@k to near-zero

Manual label-invariance validation: 690 sampled variants (30/technique), 8 of 23 techniques excluded (label preservation <=80%); among 15 retained techniques, <1.5% of adversarial claims flipped the original True/False label
Dev-set Macro F1 of AFC classifiers: FEVER Gold-Evidence 0.953+/-0.001, FEVER Claim-Only 0.826+/-0.002, FEVEROUS Gold-Evidence 0.910+/-0.002, FEVEROUS Claim-Only 0.661+/-0.002
baselines_compared: Word Swap (10% of tokens, min 1 swap)
